core_focus: Algorithms that learn patterns and models from data without explicit programming.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Datasets/Features; M: Learning Algorithm (e.g., SVM, Decision Tree); O: Predictive Model/Function; W: Loss Function Minimization; R: Training/Validation Pipeline.

core_focus: A subfield of ML using deep neural networks for representation learning and complex function approximation.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Raw Data (Image, Text, Audio); M: Neural Architecture (CNN, RNN, Transformer); O: Deep Representations/Predictions; W: Gradient-Based Optimization; R: Pre-training/Fine-tuning, Optimizer Selection.

core_focus: Learning optimal behavior through trial-and-error interactions with an environment to maximize cumulative reward.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Environment State; M: Policy/Value Function; O: Action; W: Cumulative Reward Maximization; R: Exploration-Exploitation Algorithm (e.g., PPO, DQN).

core_focus: Representing probabilistic dependencies among variables using graphs and inferring cause-effect relationships.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Observational/Interventional Data; M: Bayesian Network/Structural Causal Model; O: Probabilistic Inference/Causal Effect Estimate; W: Likelihood Maximization/Structure Learning Criterion; R: MCMC Sampling/Variational Inference.

core_focus: Learning continuously from a non-stationary stream of data while retaining previously acquired knowledge.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Sequential Data/Task Streams; M: Architectures with Memory/Regularization; O: Updated Model; W: Task Loss + Forgetting Prevention; R: Replay, Regularization, or Architectural Expansion Strategies.

core_focus: Enabling machines to 'see' and understand visual information like images and videos.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Pixel Matrix; M: Vision Model (ResNet, ViT); O: Bounding Boxes, Segmentation Maps, Captions; W: Task-Specific Visual Loss; R: Large-Scale Visual Pre-training.

core_focus: Enabling machines to understand, generate, and manipulate human language.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Text Sequences; M: Language Model (BERT, GPT); O: Translation, Summary, Answer; W: Language Modeling Objective; R: Self-Supervised Pre-training + Instruction Tuning.

core_focus: Processing and analyzing speech signals, including recognition, synthesis, and speaker identification.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Waveforms/Spectrograms; M: Acoustic Model (TDNN, Conformer); O: Transcript/Synthesized Speech; W: CTC/Seq2Seq Loss; R: End-to-End Training Pipeline.

core_focus: Integrating and jointly understanding multiple modalities like text, image, and audio.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Aligned Multimodal Data Pairs; M: Cross-Modal Fusion Architecture; O: Cross-Modal Retrieval/Generation; W: Contrastive/Generative Loss; R: Two-Tower or Fusion Network Training.

core_focus: Specializing in creating novel content (images, text, code, video, etc.).
rdr_perspective_example: I: Noise/Conditioning Prompt; M: Generative Architecture (Diffusion, GAN, Autoregressive); O: High-Fidelity Generated Sample; W: Distribution Matching Loss; R: Multi-Stage Training & Guided Sampling.

core_focus: Studying physical agents (robots) that perceive and act in the real world.
rdr_perspective_example: S: Sensor Suite; B: Body Morphology; J: Joint Commands; A: Skills/Trajectories; E: Task Environment.

core_focus: A specialized instance of robotics focusing on perception, planning, and control for ground vehicles.
rdr_perspective_example: S: Onboard Multi-Sensor Array; B: Vehicle Dynamics Model; J: Throttle/Brake/Steering; A: Driving Policy; E: Road & Traffic Scenario.

core_focus: Studying strategic decision-making, multi-agent collaboration, and competition in virtual environments.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Game State; M: Game Policy Network; O: Game Action; W: Win Rate/Score Maximization; R: Self-Play/Curriculum Learning.

core_focus: Systems that perceive, reason, plan, and act autonomously to achieve goals, often using tools and memory.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Perceptual Input & Goal Specification; M: Planning/Reasoning Module (e.g., LLM-based); O: Action/Tool Call; W: Task Success/Optimal Planning; R: Agent Loop (Think-Act-Reflect) with Memory.

core_focus: Engineering problems in efficient training, deployment, and maintenance of large-scale AI models and systems.
rdr_perspective_example: I: Compute Hardware/Workload; M: Distributed Framework/Compiler; O: Optimized Model/Service; W: Throughput Maximization/Latency Minimization; R: Parallelization Strategy/Service Orchestration.

core_focus: Brain-inspired computing, Spiking Neural Networks, neuromorphic engineering.
Ensuring AI systems are robust, reliable, controllable, and aligned with human intent and values.
Making AI decision-making transparent and auditable; detecting and mitigating algorithmic bias.
Automating the ML pipeline: feature engineering, model selection, hyperparameter optimization.
Theoretical foundations: generalization theory, optimization theory, computational complexity in ML.
Accelerating scientific discovery (e.g., in molecular simulation, astronomy, materials) using AI methods.
Formally representing world knowledge and performing logical reasoning (closely tied to Symbolic AI).
'Learning to learn': designing models that can quickly adapt to new tasks with few examples.
Learning rich representations from unlabeled data by defining a pretext task.
Training models across decentralized devices while preserving data privacy.
Learning on graph-structured data for node/edge classification, link prediction, and graph generation.
Large-scale pre-trained models that can be adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks.
Creating smaller, synthetic datasets that retain the performance of larger datasets for training models.
Identifying rare or unusual patterns in data that do not conform to expected behavior.
Transferring knowledge from a large, complex model (teacher) to a smaller, simpler model (student) while retaining performance.
